repo_prior_art.cite: doc/API.md Sec. 3.1 t3_thm: common-effect interval uses sSup of pointwise Manski lower endpoints over covariate sets

published_prior_art.cite: FangSantos2019 Eq. (13)/(36) and directional delta method for max-type nonsmooth maps; ChernozhukovLeeRosen2013 covers sup/inf intersection-bound endpoints

FangSantos2019 Theorem 3.1/Corollary 3.1: standard bootstrap consistency iff full differentiability under Gaussian first-stage limits; the local max example covers the same bootstrap-failure mechanism

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The finite two-coordinate contact derivative and local max law are a direct max-map directional-delta specialization already covered by FangSantos2019 and standard intersection-bound endpoint theory.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The centered-bootstrap failure at a locally tied max is subsumed by FangSantos2019 Theorem 3.1/Corollary 3.1; the partial-ID lower-bound notation does not create a new kernel.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The checklist omits Andrews2000, the Econometrica boundary-bootstrap inconsistency paper explicitly cited by FangSantos2019 as a max/boundary precursor.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The negative result attacks a generic centered naive bootstrap, but does not name a published intersection-bound estimator or workflow that actually recommends that naive calibration.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The phrase 'along subsequences on which the sign of D_n is positive/negative' is not a deterministic subsequence condition; state the random conditional probability-kernel limit piecewise in D_n instead.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the failure-only kernel with a new calibration or correction rule for the contact-gap endpoint, and prove uniform coverage beyond the Fang-Santos alternative bootstrap template.
If retaining a negative result, target a named published intersection-bound implementation and quote the exact step where centered naive bootstrap calibration is used.
Add Andrews2000 and the Fang-Santos max/local-path example as explicit comparators, then state precisely what remains outside those results.

recommended_next_step: Pivot: the current kernel is a routine Fang-Santos max/bootstrap instantiation, so revive it only by adding a genuinely new correction or a named-workflow-specific failure theorem.
